event: Received 10 fractions of palliative RT to the vagina
Percutaneous nephrostomy (PCN) stents placed 4 months ago
Chronic kidney disease due to impingement of vaginal vault mass on both ureters
History of acute renal failure due to underlying tumour (post-renal acute kidney failure)
Doing well at home until one week before admission
Purple urinary bag in one PCN stent
Catheter bag had <50 mL of urine output
Family noticed colour change 3 days ago
High white blood cell count of 16,760/mm3
No trace of ingestion of medications/food items that might alter urine colour
Started on empirical IV cefoperazone and sulbactam combination
Urine culture grew Escherichia coli >10^5 CFU/mL
Clinically showed no improvement due to disease burden
Decided for no aggressive treatment and preferred only palliative intent
Expired a day after her discharge
